items.q: Which type of muscle is under voluntary control?
items.why: Students think all muscles can be controlled if we try hard enough. Cardiac and smooth muscle are involuntary - you can't consciously beat your heart. Only skeletal muscle responds to conscious commands.

Students lump all training together. Plyometrics specifically targets POWER - explosive jumps and bounds use rapid eccentric-then-concentric muscle action, training the stretch-shortening cycle for fast force production.

items.q: What is the rough formula for maximum heart rate?
items.why: Students misremember the formula. 220 - age is the classic estimate (rough; varies individually). A 20-year-old's estimated max HR is around 200 bpm; an 80-year-old's is around 140.
The answer examiners want is: 1896. “1776” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's 1863. “1840” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Year of the first modern Olympic Games?
items.why: The real figure is 1896. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
